free_text: C12(gamma,p)B11 Angular Distributions in and Just
Above the Giant Resonance
Photon energy was determined by assuming
that the detected proton is from 12C(g,p0)11B.
A 2.59-mg/cm2 Trycite polystyrene (CH2) film target
10.3 cm high by 3.05 cm wide was placed 2.5 m from
the internal bremsstrahlung target of an electron
synchrotron. The target was oriented with its normal
rotated an angle of 60 degrees from the beam axis.
The cross section of the x-ray beam was 2.4x4.7 cm2
at the position of the polystyrene target.
Each detector telescope consisted of a
110-microm-thick, 3-cm2-area transmission-type
surface-barrier detector followed by a 4.5-mm-thick.,
5-cm2-area stopping detector of the lithium-drifted
silicon type. Detectors were operated at room
temperature.
The energy and angular distributions of
photoprotons have been observed with 30.5- and
32.1-MeV electron bremsstrahlung using the dExE
coincidence technique.
BP
The cross section shown here is numerically
equal to the coefficient 4pi A0. While differential
cross sections were in counts/50 keV divided by the
bremsstrahlung numbers spectrum which was normalized
to unity at 22.3 MeV.
Statistical errors are shown in the figures.
Electron bremsstrahlung energy endpoint 30.5 MeV.

Data have been grouped into 200-keV intervals to
emphasize smallness of systematic errors.
Energy spectra were first recorded at eight angular
degrees, during the runs with bremsstrahlung of
endpoint energy Ep=32.1 MeV.
Statistical errors are shown in the figures.
